_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Ureteral stricture is a narrowing of the lumen of the ureter, the duct that carries urine from the kidneys to the bladder, resulting in an obstruction.
Ureteral stricture may arise from a variety of causes and is characterized as either anastomotic or nonanastomotic, depending on how they develop.
Difficulty, straining or pain when urinating
Increased urge to urinate or more-frequent urination

preauth.label: Clinical notes including evaluation findings and planned line of treatment
Intravenous pyelogram /CT- Intravenous pyelogram +/- Micturating Cysto-Urethrogram

ppd_preauth.q: Was the Clinical notes including evaluation findings and planned line of treatment submitted?
Was the Intravenous pyelogram /CT- Intravenous pyelogram +/- Micturating Cysto-Urethrogram submitted?
Intravenous pyelogram /CT- Intravenous pyelogram +/- Micturating Cysto-Urethrogram finding detect structural abnormalities of ureteric segment?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed indoor case papers with daily vitals and treatment details available?
Was the Detailed Procedure / Operative Notes submitted?
Was the Detailed discharge summary submitted?

common_queries: Intravenous pyelogram /CT- Intravenous pyelogram +/- Micturating Cysto-Urethrogram findings do not detect structural abnormalities of the ureteric segment.
Diagnosis of ureteric stricture not backed by clinical manifestation as per clinical key pointers.
Clinical notes including evaluation findings and planned line of treatment are missing or incomplete at pre-authorization.
Intravenous pyelogram /CT- Intravenous pyelogram +/- Micturating Cysto-Urethrogram report is missing or incomplete at pre-authorization.
Detailed Procedure / Operative Notes are missing or incomplete at claim submission.

clinical_key_pointers: Proceed with Boari flap for ureteric stricture only if diagnosis made is backed by clinical manifestation
Boari flap is a useful option when the diseased segment of the ureter is too long, or ureteral mobility is too limited to perform a primary ureteroureterostomy.
